output.final_deliverable:
Exploration summary and missing items (evidence-limited)

1) Search performed (exact query string found in merged results):
   - Query: "recent news article climate change \"climate change\" (news OR article) site:reuters.com OR site:bbc.com OR site:nytimes.com OR site:guardian.co.uk OR site:washingtonpost.com"
   - Results count reported: 10

2) News-article candidates (exact URLs and titles returned by the search results sample):
   1. Title: "Climate and Environment - The New York Times"
      URL: https://www.nytimes.com/section/climate
   2. Title: "Reuters Climate Change News | Today's Latest Stories | Reuters"
      URL: https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-change/

3) Selected starting article visited (as recorded in merged results) and visit attempt(s):
   - Attempted visit URL (from merged visit attempts): https://www.reuters.com/business/environment/
   - Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized (authentication required)
     - Exact error string from merged results: "HTTP 401 Unauthorized (authentication required): HTTP fetch failed: https://www.reuters.com/business/environment/ status=401"
   - Additional merged results show similar failed visit attempts to the same URL with the note: "Site blocked: HTTP 401 Unauthorized (authentication required)"

4) What I could extract from the merged results (explicit facts found):
   - Search results sample included two URLs (listed above).
   - The exact search query used (listed above).
   - A visit to https://www.reuters.com/business/environment/ was attempted and returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized.

5) What is missing (items required by the mandate but not available in the merged results):
   - The content of a specific news article about climate change (the article text and its outbound links were not retrieved).
   - Any outbound links from that article (so I cannot identify or extract:
       a) a scientific research paper or study URL or its bibliographic details; 
       b) a government policy document URL or its title/contents; 
       c) an organization's position statement URL or its text).
   - Summaries of the scientific paper, government policy document, and organization position statement (no content was retrieved to summarize).
   - Explanations of how each linked item relates to the original article (cannot be produced without the article and its links).

6) Exploration path (as recorded):
   1. Performed a search with the exact query (see item 1).
   2. Received search result sample listing at least two candidate pages (NYTimes climate section and Reuters climate-change page) (see item 2).
   3. Attempted to visit https://www.reuters.com/business/environment/ (merged visit action) but received HTTP 401 Unauthorized and could not retrieve page content or outbound links (see item 3).
   4. Because the visit failed, subsequent steps (following links from the article to a scientific paper, government policy, and organization statement) could not be completed.

7) Next steps I can take if you want me to continue (choose one or more):
   - Retry starting from one of the search-result URLs that were returned (I can attempt to visit https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-change/ or https://www.nytimes.com/section/climate) and extract outbound links. Note: merged results do not include successful visits to those specific URLs, so a visit attempt would be new.
   - Use a different news source from the search results sample or a user-provided article URL.
   - If you want immediate results without further browsing, I can list likely candidate scientific studies, policy documents, and organizational positions based on general knowledge — but per instructions I must only use evidence present in the merged results, so I cannot do that here.

8) URLs referenced in this report (explicit list, in order):
   1. https://www.nytimes.com/section/climate
   2. https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-change/
   3. (attempted visit) https://www.reuters.com/business/environment/  -- returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized

9) Summary statement required by the mandate:
   - I was able to find candidate news-article pages via the search (two explicit URLs above) and attempted to visit a Reuters environment page, but the merged results show the visit was blocked (HTTP 401). Because the article content and its outbound links were not retrieved, I could not locate or extract the three requested linked items (scientific paper, government policy document, organization position statement). The specific missing items are listed in section 5 above.

If you want me to continue now, tell me which starting URL to try (use one of the three URLs above or provide another news-article URL) and I will attempt to visit it and follow outbound links. If you prefer a different news outlet, name it and I will start there.
output.action_summary: I searched using the exact query returned in the merged results and found two candidate news pages (NYTimes climate section and Reuters climate-change page). I then attempted to visit a Reuters environment page (https://www.reuters.com/business/environment/) but the merged visit actions show the request was blocked with HTTP 401 Unauthorized. Because the article content and outbound links were not retrieved in the merged results, I could not find or summarize a linked scientific paper, government policy document, or organization position statement. I listed the exact URLs found and the error encountered, documented the exploration path, and noted precisely what is missing. If you want me to continue, tell me which URL to try next or provide a news-article URL to start from.

merged_results.title: Search for a recent news article about climate change

result.query: recent news article climate change "climate change" (news OR article) site:reuters.com OR site:bbc.com OR site:nytimes.com OR site:guardian.co.uk OR site:washingtonpost.com

results.title: Climate and Environment - The New York Times
results.description: Our climate reporter Raymond Zhong describes America’s shifting relationship with polar research amid the threat of rising sea levels. By Raymond Zhong, Chang W.

results.title: Reuters Climate Change News | Today's Latest Stories | Reuters
results.description: Fiji and Tuvalu will host key meetings in the lead-up to the COP31 climate summit in 2026, the Pacific Islands Forum said on Thursday.

results.title: Climate - BBC News
results.description: All the latest content about Climate from the BBC.

results.title: Reuters Environmental & Climate News | Reuters
results.description: Load more articles · Spain's wild horses in peril, despite key role in wildfire prevention · The Great British Sewage Dump · China scientists rush to climate-proof potatoes · How South Korea's largest and deadliest wildfire spread · Gaza’s ...

results.title: See how climate change is accelerating - Washington Post
results.description: “There is greater acceptance now that there is a detectable acceleration of warming,” said Zeke Hausfather, a climate scientist and the research lead at the payments company Stripe. Even as the United States languishes under a frigid cold snap, the rest of the world is still experiencing unusually warm temperatures. Nuuk, Greenland, for example, saw temperatures in January more than 20 degrees Fahrenheit above average. Parts of Australia, meanwhile, have seen temperatures push past 120 degrees Fahrenheit amid a record heat wave. Some of that change was predicted by climate models.

results.title: Climate Change Is Fueling Extremes, Both Hot and Cold - The New York Times
results.description: Australia is reeling from a record heat wave that has pushed temperatures past 120 degrees Fahrenheit, or about 49 Celsius, in some areas, leading to fires and power outages. In central Africa, brutal heat has shattered records in recent days, ...

results.title: What is climate change? A really simple guide
results.description: In addition, the world's 11 warmest ... Meteorological Organization (WMO). The year 2024 was Earth's hottest ever recorded, with climate change mainly responsible for the high temperatures....

results.title: Trump Repeals Key Greenhouse Gas Finding, Erasing EPA’s Power to Fight Climate Change - The New York Times
results.description: President Trump on Thursday announced he was erasing the scientific finding that climate change endangers human health and the environment, ending the federal government’s legal authority to control the pollution that is dangerously heating ...

results.title: Nature Retracts Study Predicting Catastrophic Climate Toll - The New York Times
results.description: On Wednesday, Nature retracted it, adding to the debate on the extent of climate change’s toll on society.

results.title: U.S. Tells International Energy Agency to Drop Its Focus on Climate Change - The New York Times
results.description: Wright’s remarks were the latest effort by the Trump administration to persuade other countries and international organizations to scale back efforts to address climate change and instead to continue to rely on fossil fuels like oil, gas and coal, the main drivers of rising global temperatures.

4a4340e6-a394-4046-999a-dda56e8bdda7.title: Search for a recent news article about climate change

details.query: recent news article climate change "climate change" (news OR article) site:reuters.com OR site:bbc.com OR site:nytimes.com OR site:guardian.co.uk OR site:washingtonpost.com
details.intent?: Find one clear, recent news article focused on climate change (not an opinion piece or longform explainer) that includes outbound links to source material (papers, government docs, org statements).
target_facts?: URL of a candidate news article
Short snippet showing outbound links present (if visible in snippet)
verification_steps?: Prefer articles from reputable outlets (Reuters, BBC, NYTimes, The Guardian, Washington Post).

Verify snippet/metadata includes mention of a study, government action, or organization (phrases like 'study finds', 'according to a report by', 'the government said', 'the NGO stated') so the article is likely to include links.
Return at least 1-2 candidate article URLs ranked by likelihood of containing outbound links to the three target types.
details.extraction_intent: Produce 1 chosen article URL (primary), up to 2 alternates. For each: capture the headline, publisher, date, and the short snippet showing outbound references so the next node can visit the primary article.
details.rationale: A search node must produce candidate article URLs. This reduces uncertainty by ensuring we start from a news article that actually links to external research, policy, and organizational positions. Returning a small set (1 primary + alternates) keeps subsequent visits efficient.
details.goal: Search for a recent news article about climate change
details.original_goal: Search for a recent news article about climate change
details.parent_goal: Start at a news article about climate change (find one via search). From that article, follow links to find: (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement. For each, provide the URL, a summary of the content, and explain how it relates to the original article. Document your exploration path.

5af50ec7-cff2-4097-9b83-37693449dff7.title: Visit the selected news article and extract outbound links and context

details.intent?: Open the selected news article (the URL above is a fallback/section page if the search returns a different article; at execution time this node will visit the exact chosen_article_url returned by the search node). Extract the article's full text, its outbound links, and any inline citations to studies, government documents, or organizational statements.
target_facts?: _links_inline (list) with exact URLs and link text as presented on the page
Any explicit mentions of study titles, report names, DOI numbers, governmental report titles, or organization names
Anchor contexts (sentence or paragraph around each outbound link)
details.extraction_plan?: 1) Load article HTML and capture first 6,000 characters of main content (headline, byline, date, body). 2) Extract all outbound links found within the article body; format them as an array preserving the exact URL string as given on the page. 3) For each outbound link, capture the surrounding sentence/paragraph (for context) and classify link type (likely 'scientific paper', 'government document', 'organization statement', or 'other') based on link text and URL domain.
verification_steps?: Confirm that _links_inline contains full HTTP/HTTPS URLs (copy exact strings).
If multiple candidate links of the same inferred_type exist, mark the top 1-2 by proximity (closest sentence) to the phrase mentioning the source.
If no direct links to a target type are present, capture named references (e.g., study title or agency report name) so we can search for them in a follow-up search.
details.rationale: We must extract the exact outbound URLs from the news article to visit the scientific paper, government policy document, and organization's position statement. The visit node must capture exact URL strings (per requirements) and context so subsequent visits use precise links and we can explain how each source relates to the article.
details.goal: Visit the selected news article and extract outbound links and context
details.original_goal: Visit the selected news article and extract outbound links and context
details.parent_goal: Start at a news article about climate change (find one via search). From that article, follow links to find: (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement. For each, provide the URL, a summary of the content, and explain how it relates to the original article. Document your exploration path.

c035054e-8715-4872-9e03-eaee72f22230.title: Visit the scientific research paper or study linked from the article

details.intent?: At runtime, replace details.url with the exact URL taken from the news article's _links_inline entry classified as 'scientific paper' (copy exact URL). Extract the paper's bibliographic metadata and key findings/abstract. If the link points to a publisher landing page (Nature, Science, PNAS, etc.) or to a PDF/DOI, capture DOI, title, authors, abstract, and any available summary or press-release content.
target_facts?: abstract or summary (string, up to 6000 chars)
key findings cited (extract sentences that align with the news article's statements)
details.extraction_plan?: 1) Visit the exact URL from the article's links. 2) If the page is a publisher landing page, extract title, authors, abstract, DOI, and PDF link if accessible; if the link is a PDF, parse first page for title and abstract. 3) Locate statements that match the news article's claims (e.g., specific numbers, trends) and extract those sentences for cross-checking.
measurable_output_fields: evidence_relation_to_article (text explaining which claims in the article are supported by which paper excerpts)
verification_steps?: Check DOI resolves or matches publisher metadata.
Confirm extracted key findings contain identical numerical claims/phrasing (or note paraphrase).
If the link redirects to a summary or press release rather than the paper, capture that and note the difference.
details.rationale: A direct visit to the paper is needed to verify the news article's use of scientific evidence. This node reduces uncertainty by producing verifiable bibliographic data and exact excerpts connecting the paper to the article's claims.
details.goal: Visit the scientific research paper or study linked from the article
details.original_goal: Visit the scientific research paper or study linked from the article
details.parent_goal: Start at a news article about climate change (find one via search). From that article, follow links to find: (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement. For each, provide the URL, a summary of the content, and explain how it relates to the original article. Document your exploration path.

e95183b1-53c0-446d-a89e-4f1b6943a987.title: Visit the government policy document linked from the article

details.intent?: At runtime, this node will visit the exact URL from the article's _links_inline entry classified as 'government' (copy exact URL). Extract the document title, issuing agency, publication date, executive summary, and key policy points referenced by the article.
target_facts?: policy_actions or recommendations (list of extracted bullet points or sentences)
details.extraction_plan?: 1) Visit the exact government doc URL. 2) If it's a PDF, extract title and executive summary; if an HTML page, extract headline, lead paragraphs, and any downloadable PDF links. 3) Identify the sections the news article references and extract those paragraphs to show direct relation.
measurable_output_fields: evidence_relation_to_article (text linking article claims to specific doc excerpts)
verification_steps?: Confirm issuing domain matches an official government domain (.gov, .gov.uk, or equivalent).
Check publication date aligns with the timing referred to in the article.
If the article references a press release rather than a formal policy, capture that difference.
details.rationale: Government documents are authoritative sources for policy claims. Visiting the exact document reduces uncertainty about whether the article accurately represents government actions or plans.
details.goal: Visit the government policy document linked from the article
details.original_goal: Visit the government policy document linked from the article
details.parent_goal: Start at a news article about climate change (find one via search). From that article, follow links to find: (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement. For each, provide the URL, a summary of the content, and explain how it relates to the original article. Document your exploration path.

1b80d291-0678-4ef1-81ae-f1469c64e6df.title: Visit the organization's position statement linked from the article

details.intent?: At runtime, visit the exact URL from the article's _links_inline entry classified as 'organization statement' (copy exact URL). This could be an NGO statement, industry group position, or corporate response. Extract the statement text, date, and the organization's stated position relevant to the article.
details.extraction_plan?: 1) Visit the exact URL provided in the article links. 2) Extract the full text of the statement or press release and identify the specific claims or recommendations the news article cited. 3) Capture whether the statement is a reaction/endorsement/critique and any calls to action.
measurable_output_fields: evidence_relation_to_article (text explaining how the article used or characterized the organization's statement)
verification_steps?: Confirm the domain belongs to the named organization.
Check that the excerpt the article uses is present verbatim or paraphrased in the statement.
If the article paraphrased selectively, note differences.
details.rationale: Organizational statements often contextualize news stories. Visiting the statement ensures we can compare the article's characterization with the organization's actual words and avoid misrepresentation.
details.goal: Visit the organization's position statement linked from the article
details.original_goal: Visit the organization's position statement linked from the article
